zh.Limerick, York, Maine: 美國緬因州約克利默里克
zh.lifeSummary: Nancy Chadwell是Moses Chadwell和?的女兒，於1772年出生在美國緬因州約克利默里克。她於1798年8月21日與Lemuel Drew在美國緬因州約克利默里克結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1828年6月28日在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可劍橋大學默里 利默里克享年56歲，埋葬在美國緬因州阿盧斯圖可劍橋大學默里 利默里克劍橋大學默里 利默里克 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Chadwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Caldwell
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places in England and Scotland, variously spelled, that are named with Old English ceald ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Caldwell in North Yorkshire is one major source of the surname; Caldwell in Renfrewshire in Scotland another. Possibly also from Caldwell (Warwickshire), Caldwall (Worcestershire), Cauldwell (Bedfordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire), Caudle Green (Gloucestershire), Caudle Ditch or Cawdle Fen (Cambridgeshire), Chadwell (Essex, Hertfordshire, Leicestershire, Wiltshire), Chardwell (Essex), or Chardle Ditch (Cambridgeshire, early recorded as Kadewelle).

Irish: when not the English surname, this is an Anglicized form of Ó Fuarghuis or Ó hUarghusa ‘descendant of (F)uarghus’, a personal name whose literal sense ‘cold’ + ‘choice’ was reinterpreted as coming from fuaruisce ‘cold water’.

History: Several Caldwells emigrated from Scotland to America by way of Ireland in the 18th century. James Caldwell (1734–81), a son of settler John Caldwell, was born in Charlotte County, VA, and was a militant clergyman during the revolutionary war. Andrew Caldwell, a Scottish farmer, emigrated to North America in 1718 and started a family in Lancaster County, PA. His son David was a Presbyterian clergyman and well-known revolutionary war patriot.

nameMeanings.name: Nancy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin. From the 18th century it is clearly used as a pet form of   Ann   (  see  Nan  ), but it may originally have been a similar formation deriving from the common medieval given name   Annis  , a vernacular form of   Agnes .  Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

variants.name: Nancie
meanings: Of uncertain origin. Nowadays it is an independent name, and was especially popular in America in the 1930s, 40s, and 50s.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries: 1775: Lincoln, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Lincoln, Massachusetts, United States 1790: Washington, Massachusetts, United States 1820, Washington, Maine, United States 1839: Aroostook, Maine, United States
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The Making of the U.S. Constitution.
historicEvents.description: The Philadelphia Convention was intended to be the first meeting to establish the first system of government under the Articles of Confederation. From this Convention, the Constitution of the United States was made and then put into place making it one of the major events in all American History.

historicEvents.title: The First Presidential Election
historicEvents.description: The First Presidential election was held in the newly created United States of America. Under the Articles of Confederation, the executive branch of the country was not set up for an individual to help lead the nation. So, under the United States Constitution they position was put in. Because of his prominent roles during the Revolutionary War, George Washington was voted in unanimously as the First President of the United States.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Whitehead Light
historicEvents.description: In 1804, President Thomas Jefferson authorized the creation of a light station on Whitehead Island. The light house went into service by 1807. It is the third-oldest light house in Maine. Whitehead Light still exists as the private property of Pine Island Camp, a non-profit organization.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.
Oldest Grave seen in the Memorials list.

en.lifeSummary: Nancy Chadwell was born in 1772, in Limerick, York, Maine, United States as the daughter of Moses Chadwell and ?. She married Lemuel Drew on 21 August 1798, in Limerick, York, Maine, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 28 June 1828, in New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United States, at the age of 56, and was buried in New Limerick Cemetery, New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Nancy Chadwellは1772年にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークリメリックでMoses Chadwellと?の娘として生まれた。1798年8月21日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州ヨークリメリックでLemuel Drewと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1828年6月28日にアメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクニュー リメリックで56歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国メイン州アルーストオクニュー リメリックニュー リメリック セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nancy Chadwell(은)는 1772년 미국 메인 주 요르크 라임릭에서 Moses Chadwell(와)과 ?의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1798년 8월 21일 미국 메인 주 요르크 라임릭에서 Lemuel Drew(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1828년 6월 28일 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 메인 주 아루스투크 뉴 라임릭 뉴 라임릭 세멧리에 묻혔다.

ru.New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine: ню лимирикк, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты
ru.New Limerick Cemetery--New Limerick, Aro: ню лимирикк кеметири, ню лимирикк, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Nancy Chadwell родилась в 1772 г. в местности лимирикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. Её родителей звали Moses Chadwell и ?. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lemuel Drew 21 августа 1798 г. в местности лимирикк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 28 июня 1828 г. в местности ню лимирикк, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности ню лимирикк кеметири, ню лимирикк, арустук, Мэн, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Nancy Chadwell nació en 1772, en Limerick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos como hija de Moses Chadwell y ?. Se casó con Lemuel Drew el 21 de agosto de 1798, en Limerick, York, Maine, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 28 de junio de 1828, en New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultada en New Limerick Cemetery, New Limerick, Aroostook, Maine, Estados Unidos.
